title: xylophyllos gaudichaudii
intro: Xylophyllos gaudichaudii, also known as the Chilean rosewood or Chilean rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a tree native to Chile, traditionally used for its medicinal properties and valued for its essential oil, which is rich in limonene and other terpenes. The essential oil extracted from Xylophyllos gaudichaudii has been found to possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can help to treat various health issues, such as skin infections, wounds, and respiratory problems. Xylophyllos gaudichaudii is often used in herbal preparations, including essential oil, extracts, and tinctures, which can be applied topically or ingested under the guidance of a healthcare professional to promote overall well-being.
